title: Laser Scribing Automation & Machine Vision Integration
description: Automated the laser scribing process for perovskite solar modules, integrating machine vision for automatic positional alignment and skew correction. Achieved 500% throughput increase and improved positional repeatability from 60μm to 20μm, enabling transition from engineer to technician operation.

title: World-Record Perovskite Module Efficiency
description: Led all loss mechanisms related to perovskite solar cell modulization. Achieved world-record 24% efficiency through joint development with NREL, driving a 34% improvement in initial module efficiency and 35× improvement in degradation lifespan.

title: Company-Wide Defect Tracking System
description: Identified two defects present on 50% of modules causing 20× degradation impact compared to other defects. Wrote functional specification for the software team and led company-wide rollout, enabling large-scale data collection to guide deliberate improvements.

title: Non-Invasive Intracranial Pressure Measurement Prototype
description: Developed a user-friendly prototype testbed to evaluate a groundbreaking non-invasive method of measuring intracranial pressure via ultrasound in patients who have recently suffered acute neurologic injury.

title: F-35 Ground Equipment Cost-Benefit Analysis
company: National Security Innovation Network, U.S. Air Force
description: Lead author of a cost-benefit analysis on stationing aerospace ground equipment at Gila Bend Auxiliary Airfield to prepare diverted F-35 aircraft for return to Luke Air Force Base. Recommended strategy was implemented at Luke AFB with planned nationwide rollout.

title: NASA Micro-g NExT EVA Tool Attachment
description: Designed, proposed, and manufactured an EVA tool attachment mechanism for astronaut utility belts for NASA's Micro-g NExT Challenge. Utilized FEA, FMEA Risk Matrix, Compliance Matrix, and Trade Study Pugh Chart. Selected for Phase 2 testing at NASA's Neutral Buoyancy Lab.
